title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
description: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals — from avatars to cinematic portraits.

title: As Tim Cook steps down, Apple hit record sales — but a chip shortage looms
description: Cook warned that Apple is facing supply-chain headwinds from RAMageddon that could impact its business.

title: Y Combinator alum Skio sells for $105M cash, only raised $8M, founder says
description: Subscription billing fintech Skio sold to its competitor Recharge in what was a healthy exit, according to its founder and former CEO.

title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
description: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI company’s latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.

title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
description: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.

title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
description: The two wildly fast-growing rivals have raised massive sums, pushed into each other's home turf, and now have dueling ad campaigns.

title: Rivian downsizes DOE loan to $4.5B for Georgia factory
description: Rivian has reworked its loan deal with the Department of Energy and now expects to borrow $4.5 billion to build its new factory in Georgia, down from the original amount of $6.6 billion.

title: Hackers are actively exploiting a bug in cPanel, used by millions of websites
description: Web hosts are scrambling to fix the bug under active attack by hackers. One company said hackers have been abusing the bug for months.

title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
description: OpenAI will begin rolling out its cybersecurity testing tool, GPT-5.5 Cyber only "to critical cyber defenders" at first.

title: EV startup Faraday Future paid $7.5M to company tied to founder Jia Yueting
description: The perpetually struggling EV company made the payments while being investigated by the SEC. That four-year probe was ultimately closed in March.

title: OpenAI announces new advanced security for ChatGPT accounts, including a partnership with Yubico
description: OpenAI is launching additional opt-in protections for ChatGPT accounts. The new security initiative includes a new partnership with security key provider Yubico.

title: Elon Musk testifies that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models
description: "Distillation" is a hot topic as frontier labs try to prevent smaller competitors from copying their models.

title: FDA approval, fundraising, and the reality of building in healthcare according to BioticsAI founder
description: BioticsAI CEO Robhy Bustami joined Isabelle Johannessen on Build Mode to discuss how the company has navigated a highly regulated space and kept the team motivated while cutting through all the red tape.

title: Google’s Gemini AI assistant is hitting the road in millions of vehicles
description: The move signals Google’s push to bring more advanced, conversational AI into the driving experience.

title: TikTok’s new ‘Campus Hub’ features college group chats and feeds
description: The new hub features dedicated college group chats and personalized feeds designed to help students stay connected with their campus communities, even while they’re away for the summer.

title: Stripe introduces Link, a digital wallet that autonomous AI agents can use, too
description: Link lets users connect cards, banks, and subscriptions, then authorize AI agents to spend securely via approval flows.

title: Salesforce is crowdsourcing its AI roadmap — with customers
description: Salesforce lets its customers lead its product roadmap with the thinking that if one enterprise customer has a problem, the others likely do too.

title: Uber taps Hertz to clean, charge, and fix its Lucid Motors robotaxis
description: Hertz is creating a new affiliate company called "Oro Mobility" to provide fleet management solutions "across a range of mobility segments."

title: SpaceX backer 137 Ventures raises $700M for two growth-stage funds
description: VC firm 137 Ventures has raised over $700 million to back growth-stage startups. Its portfolio includes SpaceX, Anduril, Hadrian.

title: Dental practice software maker fixes bug that exposed patients’ medical records
description: Exclusive: The security bug is now fixed, but the patient who found it said it was challenging to alert the software company about the issue.

title: Spotify introduces verified artist badges to help distinguish humans from AI
description: As AI-generated artists and tracks flood music streaming platforms, Spotify is rolling out a new “Verified by Spotify” badge to help listeners more easily

title: Netflix wants you to watch ‘Clips,’ its TikTok-like vertical video feed
description: Netflix is redesigning its mobile app and introducing Clips, a vertical video feed intended to help users discover new content by sharing highlights from original Netflix programming.

title: Meta says its business AI now facilitates 10 million conversations a week
description: Meta said over 8 billion advertisers have used at least one of its gen AI tools

title: Instagram cracks down on content aggregators
description: The update targets content aggregators that don't post original content and instead simply re-upload others' posts.

title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
description: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

title: Amazon, Meta join fight to end Google Pay, PhonePe dominance in India
description: PhonePe and Google Pay command 80% of India's UPI instant payments network. Rivals are set to meet with regulators to lobby for restrictions.

title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
description: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
description: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

title: On the stand, Elon Musk can’t escape his own tweets
description: Elon Musk took the stand for the second day for his attempt to legally dismantle OpenAI.

title: Meta is still burning money on AR/VR
description: Meta is losing billions on Reality Labs each quarter, and its AI expenditures are only going to increase its spending.

title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
description: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
description: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B, but says growth was capacity-constrained
description: Google Cloud topped $20B in quarterly revenue for the first time, fueled by surging demand for AI. But capacity constraints mean it could have grown even faster.

title: Google gains 25M subscriptions in Q1, driven by YouTube and Google One
description: Google added 25M paid subscriptions in Q1, reaching 350M total, as YouTube and Google One grow.

title: Fusion power startup Zap Energy pulls a partial pivot, adding nuclear fission to the mix
description: Surprise! Fusion startup Zap Energy says it will be developing fission reactors alongside its fusion devices.

title: Parallel Web Systems hits $2B valuation five months after its last big raise
description: The AI agent-tool startup founded by former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal has raised $100 million, led by Sequoia, months after raising a previous $100 million.

title: Google Photos uses AI to make the iconic closet from ‘Clueless’ a reality
description: Google says the new feature will leverage AI technology to automatically create a copy of your wardrobe that's based on the pieces of clothing appearing in your Google Photos library.

title: More Gemini features are coming to Google TV
description: Google TV just got more Gemini features, including the ability to transform photos and videos with tools Nano Banana and Veo.

title: Bill Gurley, Jack Altman back startup Pursuit, which helps companies sell to government
description: On Wednesday, Pursuit announced a $22 million Series A round led by Mike Rosengarten, the co-founder of OpenGov, with big-name VCs participating.

title: Roku’s $3 streaming service, Howdy, reaches 1M subs, per recent report
description: Roku’s $2.99 streaming service Howdy has topped 1M subscribers, showing demand for cheaper, low-commitment alternatives to pricier streamers.

title: Uber is in the hotel business now, thanks in part to AI
description: Uber announced several new features on Wednesday during its annual event, which push far beyond the company's original ride-hailing purpose and deeper into its users' lives.

title: Apple loses bid to pause App Store fee changes as case heads to Supreme Court
description: Apple lost its bid to pause court-ordered App Store payment changes, keeping external purchase links in place as its case with Epic heads toward the Supreme Court.

title: Earth AI is vertically integrating the search for critical minerals
description: When Earth AI started hitting months-long delays in its search for critical minerals, it decided to take matters into its own hands.

title: Sri Lanka discloses another missing payment, days after hackers stole $2.5M from its finance ministry
description: The government of Sri Lanka has lost more than $3 million in two recent, separate cybersecurity incidents as the country continues to recover from its 2022 debt crisis.

title: Firestorm Labs raises $82M to take drone factories into the field
description: A defense startup just raised $82 million to put drone factories inside shipping containers and bring manufacturing to the front lines.

title: BMW i Ventures has a new $300M fund and AI is riding shotgun
description: BMW i Ventures is interested in startups working on agentic AI and physical AI, as well as industrial software, advanced materials, and manufacturing and supply chain technologies.

title: Meet Shapes, the app bringing humans and AI into the same group chats
description: Think Discord chats, but with AI characters in addition to humans.

title: Google Translate now lets you practice pronunciation
description: The feature is rolling out in the U.S. and India with support for English, Spanish, and Hindi.

title: Jack Dorsey-backed Vine reboot Divine launches to the public
description: Divine, a Vine reboot backed by Jack Dorsey’s nonprofit, revives six-second looping videos.

title: Coby Adcock’s Scout AI raises $100 million to train its models for war. We visited its bootcamp.
description: We visited Scout AI's training ground where it's working on AI agents that give individual soldiers control of fleets of autonomous vehicles.

title: How one venture firm is investing in an increasingly fragmented world | TechCrunch
description: Geopolitical turmoil has made venture investing challenging, leading Kompas VC to carve out a niche in startups focused on the physical world.

title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship | TechCrunch
description: It's a story Musk has told before -- in interviews and to author Walter Isaacson for his bestselling biography of Musk -- but Tuesday was the first time he said it under oath.

title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS | TechCrunch
description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages | TechCrunch
description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

title: Match Group invests $100M in Sniffies, a cruising app for gay men | TechCrunch
description: The app is Match Group's newest attempt to get mobile users excited about online romance again.

title: Google expands Pentagon's access to its AI after Anthropic's refusal | TechCrunch
description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

title: Paragon is not collaborating with Italian authorities probing spyware attacks, report says | TechCrunch
description: Despite promising to help determine what happened with the hacks targeting journalists and activists in Italy, Israeli American spyware maker Paragon has reportedly not responded to authorities’ requests for information.

title: US Supreme Court appears split over controversial use of 'geofence' search warrants | TechCrunch
description: The U.S. top court is expected to rule on whether to allow police to identify criminal suspects by dragnet searching the databases of tech giants.

title: Australia forces Big Tech firms to pay for news or face a 2.25% tax | TechCrunch
description: Australia is giving Big Tech a simple choice. Pay journalists. Or pay the government.

title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android | TechCrunch
description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

title: Founder of Shark Tank-backed startup Scholly sues his acquirer Sallie Mae | TechCrunch
description: Chris Gray is suing his startup’s acquirer, Sallie Mae, for wrongful termination and alleging it's selling student data through a subsidiary. Sallie Mae denies the allegations and vows to fight.

title: Apple introduces a cheaper option for App Store subscriptions | TechCrunch
description: Apple is adding a new subscription option that lets app developers offer lower monthly pricing in exchange for a 12-month commitment.

title: Snapchat brings AI-powered conversational advertising to its app | TechCrunch
description: Users will be able to chat with a brand's AI agent to do things like ask questions and get recommendations.

title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers | TechCrunch
description: YouTube is rolling out the new AI search feature to Premium subscribers in the U.S. on an opt-in basis.

title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its 'mind-reading' tech for consumer wearables | TechCrunch
description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech—a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

title: Red Hat's OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer | TechCrunch
description: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that let's it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.

title: Otter's new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools | TechCrunch
description: Otter is also releasing a new Windows app that can capture meeting notes without joining one

title: Drizzle on top: a new high-end dog food brand is coming for the 1% | TechCrunch
description: Golden Child is launching with two "five-star" products: a fresh frozen meal system and, more intriguingly, a "drizzle." It has also raised $37 million in funding.

title: India's Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up | TechCrunch
description: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

title: Letterboxd, the social platform for film buffs, reportedly looking for new owner | TechCrunch
description: Potential buyers of Letterboxd include Versant, the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, and Hollywood media company The Ankler, according to Semafor.

title: Consumers lost $2.1B to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports | TechCrunch
description: The agency reports that losses from social media scams have increased eightfold and that social media scams resulted in higher losses than any other method scammers used to contact consumers.

title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal | TechCrunch
description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

title: DeepMind's David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data | TechCrunch
description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

title: Hacker who allegedly carried out cyberattacks for China is extradited to US | TechCrunch
description: Xu Zewei is accused of participating in a Chinese government hacking group that broke into thousands of U.S. organizations and stole COVID-19-related research.

title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch | TechCrunch
description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

title: Data center demand drives 66% surge in natural gas power plant costs | TechCrunch
description: Natural gas power plant costs have nearly doubled in two years and take 23% longer to build as data center electricity demand skyrockets.

title: What’s behind Europe’s efforts to ditch US software in favor of sovereign tech | TechCrunch
description: Governments across Europe are looking to rely less on American tech providers.

title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe | TechCrunch
description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps | TechCrunch
description: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.

title: Critical infrastructure giant Itron says it was hacked | TechCrunch
description: The American technology giant provides water and energy monitoring and utility meters to hundreds of millions of homes and businesses.

title: Spotify's next frontier: Fitness content | TechCrunch
description: Spotify is adding fitness as its next major category, launching workout videos, playlists, and Peloton classes inside the app for free and Premium users.

title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space | TechCrunch
description: Overview Energy's first contract with Meta is a small step toward a future of space-based solar power.

title: Truecaller faces mounting pressures as its growth matures | TechCrunch
description: As growth slows, Truecaller is leaning on subscriptions, business services, and new features to sustain momentum beyond India.

title: The Stanford freshmen who want to rule the world … will probably read this book and try even harder | TechCrunch
description: Can a book like this actually change anything? Or does the spotlight, as it always seems to, send more students racing to the place?

title: Amazon’s new podcast strategy: Monetize everything | TechCrunch
description: Amazon's podcasting business seems to have transformed over the past six months.

title: What Tim Cook built | TechCrunch
description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss how Apple has changed since Cook became CEO in 2011, and what challenges incoming CEO John Ternus will be facing.

title: TechCrunch Mobility: Elon’s admission | TechCrunch
description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.
